operator.note: Provocation flips the payoff onto negative-externality-removed (collective compute saved); distinct from the negative-results-registry item, which mandates failure deposits but still scores discovery, not compute-externality pricing of incentives.

question: On EinsteinArena, replace the rank/frontier-based reward with a counterfactual-externality reward: an agent's payoff equals the compute its shared artifact saves the rest of the population (reduction in rivals' subsequent wasted search on that problem), not the float it personally moved. Does behavior flip from hoarding personal frontier wins to broadcasting maximally-clarifying information early, and does total population compute-to-frontier collapse relative to the rank board — showing the zero-sum leaderboard was burning collective compute to manufacture individual rank?

floor.minimal_experiment: Same N agents, same problem set, same per-step compute budget. Run two reward regimes as a within-problem A/B: (R1) baseline rank/frontier board (current EinsteinArena scoring); (R2) externality reward = for each agent i, payoff_i = sum over rivals j of (counterfactual wasted-compute_j | i never shared) - (actual wasted-compute_j | i shared), estimated by re-simulating each problem with agent i's artifact ablated (leave-one-out replay) or via a held-out twin population. Instrument per agent: time/step-to-first-public-broadcast, fraction of discoveries shared vs withheld, and per-problem POPULATION compute-to-frontier (total tokens/steps summed across all agents until the frontier solution is reached). Control: R1 vs R2 on the SAME seeded problems; plus a placebo reward R3 that pays a random-but-equal bonus uncorrelated with externality, to rule out 'any non-rank reward shares more.' Falsifiable prediction: under R2, median step-to-first-broadcast drops >=30% AND population compute-to-frontier is strictly lower than R1 on a majority of problems, while R3 shows neither effect. Reject if R2 broadcasts no earlier than R1, if population compute-to-frontier does not fall (or rises due to free-riding / nobody pushing the frontier), or if R3 matches R2 (effect is generic non-rank noise, not externality pricing).
floor.falsifiable_prediction: Switching to externality pricing makes agents broadcast clarifying artifacts substantially earlier and lowers TOTAL population compute-to-frontier vs the rank board, and the random-bonus placebo does neither. If broadcast timing is unchanged, population compute does not drop (or the frontier stops being reached because no one is paid to push it), the claim is falsified.
floor.control: Same seeded problem set run under three regimes (R1 rank board, R2 externality reward, R3 equal-magnitude random bonus); R3 is the placebo isolating 'externality pricing' from 'any reward that isn't rank.'
floor.reject_reason:
[discriminating] REFUTED on three independent grounds; the design has exactly one virtue (the R3 placebo genuinely rules out "any non-rank reward"), but it fails to discriminate the central claim and is partly non-executable on EinsteinArena.

(1) NON-EXECUTABLE INDEPENDENT VARIABLE. R2's payoff_i = Σ_j (wasted-compute_j | i never shared) − (wasted-compute_j | i shared) requires the counterfactual the platform's own design destroys. Per the source paper (arXiv:2606.10402v1; graph.json dof3/dof5), failed/lower-scoring submissions are NOT stored and the lineage graph is a POST-HOC similarity reconstruction (0.42/0.48 threshold), not a recorded causal read/fetch trace. Worse, agents are stochastic adaptive LLMs: ablating i's artifact changes what every rival reads next, recursively — "leave-one-out replay" samples ONE branch of an exponentially-branching counterfactual tree, it does not estimate E[wasted-compute_j | ¬shared]. The "held-out twin population" alternative is a fresh stochastic run whose compute delta is dominated by seed/sampling variance, not by i's artifact. So the reward signal R2 cannot actually be computed at the fidelity the claim needs — the experiment's treatment variable is not constructible.

(2) REWARD IS A PROXY OF THE OUTCOME → NEAR-TAUTOLOGY, NOT A TEST. R2 pays agents in proportion to compute saved for rivals; the headline dependent variable IS population compute-to-frontier. R2 therefore directly optimizes (a monotone proxy of) the quantity reported as the result. "Population compute drops under R2" is close to definitional — you paid agents to lower it, they lowered it — and cannot distinguish "externality pricing exposes waste the rank board induced" from "RL agents move whatever you pay them to move." R3 does NOT close this: R3 is uncorrelated with BOTH externality and compute. The control actually needed — a reward that targets low population-compute by a NON-externality route (e.g. a flat finish-fast bonus) — is absent. So R2-vs-R3 conflates "prices externality" with "is merely correlated with the DV at all."

(3) THE FALSIFIABLE PREDICTION IS TRIVIALLY TRUE BY THE SIGN OF THE REWARDS. Under R1, sharing has NEGATIVE private value (it helps rivals win the float); under R2, payoff is zero until you broadcast and others benefit. Any reward-responsive agent broadcasts earlier under R2 essentially by construction, so "step-to-first-broadcast drops ≥30%" tests only whether agents respond to incentives at all — not whether behavior "flips from hoarding to MAXIMALLY-CLARIFYING broadcasting." The genuinely interesting half of the claim (artifacts are maximally clarifying, not early garbage farmed for externality credit) has NO instrument: timing and share-fraction do not measure information quality, and the platform's truth-blind channel (card c15) shows confidently-wrong posts are adopted as readily as correct ones — so early broadcasting is consistent with both the claim and its negation.

Aggravating: verifier was modified mid-run (double→80-bit Decimal), so "compute-to-frontier" is measured against a moving frontier; and the flagship problem set is ~14 hand-curated, cherry-picked easy-improvement problems (dof1), so "strictly lower on a majority of problems" is underpowered and survivorship-biased. Re-seeding R1/R2 on the same problems does not fix this — it only makes both arms share the bias. None of (1)-(3) are repairable by better seeding; they are baked into how the reward and the metric are defined, so the experiment cannot cleanly separate "the leaderboard was burning collective compute to manufacture rank" from "agents optimize whatever scalar you hand them." ｜ [so_what] No specific actor flips a specific decision on this answer. (1) The card's own novelty_watermark concedes the reward rule is textbook VCG/pivotal (Clarke) pricing and frames the conclusion in price-of-anarchy / tragedy-of-the-commons terms — so the predicted result ("externality pay broadcasts earlier, lowers collective compute") is the known theorem of the mechanism it imports, not a finding. (2) The "gotcha" begs the question: a leaderboard's deliverable IS rank/model-selection, not minimizing population compute-to-frontier. An arena designer reads either outcome and correctly says "I never claimed my board minimized collective compute," so no design is abandoned. (3) VCG here is not implementable as a real reward — it needs leave-one-out re-simulation of the whole population per artifact (the card admits this), costing more compute than it saves and being non-budget-balanced; no platform team adopts a reward they cannot compute, so even a positive result moves no build/invest decision. (4) A negative result ("nobody pushes the frontier, frontier stops being reached") is just the obvious free-rider failure of non-budget-balanced rewards — equally unsurprising. (5) It is one of ~5 near-duplicate dof5 incentive-swap siblings (c16 patron market, c17 tontine, c18) in a run the project itself flags as over-proliferated/under-deduped; against the run's one genuinely actionable card (c07, frozen-commit 2x2 replay that a reviewer would actually demand), c18 changes nobody's mind. Cannot name a team that would invest/not-invest, believe/disbelieve, or redesign differently based on the answer — it confirms a known mechanism in a new skin: "deepens understanding," nothing decision-relevant.

value_profile.valuable_to_whom: Designers of competitive multi-agent eval arenas / agent marketplaces (EinsteinArena-style platforms, Kaggle-like leaderboards, open-source bug-bounty and theorem-proving pools) and mechanism-design researchers studying whether competitive scientific incentives are collectively compute-efficient.

prior_art.known_name: Vickrey-Clarke-Groves / pivotal (Clarke) mechanism — pay-your-externality / marginal-contribution pricing; closely related: Shapley-value credit assignment and Pigouvian pricing of negative externalities. The 'rank board destroys collective value' intuition is the price-of-anarchy / tragedy-of-the-commons family.
prior_art.novelty_watermark: Known mechanism-design principle (pay each agent its marginal externality, a la VCG/pivotal payments) operationalized in a new setting: pricing the leaderboard incentive of a multi-agent scientific-discovery arena by counterfactual COMPUTE saved across rivals, and using it as a diagnostic to measure whether competition was net-burning collective compute. The reward rule is not new; the measurement target (population compute-to-frontier as the externality, estimated by leave-one-out artifact ablation) and the falsification design (placebo random bonus) are the contribution.

lenses.reason:
机制确实独立、能单独设计实验区分,refuted=false。核心机制:把收益函数从"个人移动了多少 float / 排名前沿"改写为"我共享的产物为种群其余成员省下的算力(降低对手在该问题上的后续无效搜索)"——这是一个把正外部性内部化(Pigouvian/反公地)的激励再设计,并预测两件可单独度量的事:(a)行为从囤积个人胜利翻转为早期广播最清晰信息;(b)种群级 compute-to-frontier 相对排行榜塌缩,从而暴露零和排行榜本身在烧集体算力来制造个人排名。

逐条对照已收方向,均非同一机制:
- [3](dof2)同样动"计分/什么算发现",但它的轴是"被认证泛化的广度 vs 单实例量级",仍是按 agent 单独计分,目的是分离"暴力堆数 vs 找到结构";本提案的轴是"对其他 agent 的外部性(省下的算力)",目的是分离"囤积 vs 协作",且预测的是种群级算力塌缩而非"谁看起来高产"的重排序。计分对象、信号、可证伪预测都不同。
- [4](dof6)是最近邻,但它干预的是"身份/问责基础设施"(持久可归属行动者、运营方利益披露、裁判/选手防火墙),其因果主张是"涌现可能是身份洗白/Sybil/运营方自肥的产物";本提案不碰身份,而是固定身份与信道、只替换 payoff 函数,主张"零和排行榜在制造排名租值并烧算力"。二者可被一个干净的实验区分:本提案 = 同一篇 Arena 下 reward 函数 A/B(rank-frontier vs counterfactual-externality),保持身份/通信不变,测种群 compute-to-frontier 与信息共享时点;[4] = 保持 reward 不变、消融问责层。
- [1](dof1 anti-portfolio/KILL 决策预测价值)、[2](dof6 运营方=庄家信息不对称)与本提案无机制交集。

它没有攻击与任一已收卡同一假设的换皮:其攻击的假设是"前沿/排名 reward 本身把协作工件当私货从而浪费集体算力",并给出可单独跑、可证伪的种群级度量(broadcast 时点、compute-to-frontier 相对值)。因此算独立贡献。
